title: Creamy Middle Eastern Collards Baba Ganoush
description: A quick and creamy Middle Eastern dish featuring collards in a baba ganoush form, made in a cast-iron skillet using a bain-marie method, and is macrobiotic-friendly.
instructions: Fill a large pot with water and bring it to a boil to create a bain-marie.
Reduce the heat to a simmer and place a cast-iron skillet over the pot, making sure the bottom of the skillet is not touching the water.
Add the chopped collard greens to the skillet and cook, covered, until they are tender, about 10 minutes.
Remove the skillet from the heat and let it cool slightly.
Add the lemon juice, tahini, garlic, olive oil, salt, and paprika to the skillet with the collard greens.
Blend the mixture with an immersion blender until it is smooth and creamy.
Return the skillet to the bain-marie and cook, stirring constantly, until the mixture is heated through.
Remove the skillet from the heat and serve the baba ganoush warm.
Garnish with paprika and serve with pita bread or vegetables.
